title: NASASpaceFlight.com
text:
Firefly Aerospace is returning its Alpha launch vehicle to flight after a nearly 10-month stand down following the failed FLTA006 “Message In A Booster” mission in April 2025. FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” was scheduled to launch from the California coast no earlier than 4:50 PM PST on Sunday, March 1 (00:50 UTC on March 2), but Firefly scrubbed the launch due to continued upper level winds. The company is working with the USSF Space Launch Delta 30 team to determine a new launch date.
The launch window lasted until 6:50 PM PST on March 1 (02:50 UTC on March 2). The launch had been scheduled for Friday, Feb. 27, then Saturday, Feb. 28, but was postponed both times due to forecast high upper-level winds.
